bots.summary: 12 official weather signals found. Core Hancock signals: 12 (12 SPC reports, 0 SPC outlooks, 11 hail/wind or derecho, 1 tornado, 0 tropical). Contents/water/fire signals: 0. Air-quality-only alerts are excluded.

recommendations.headline: SPC Preliminary Hail Report: 2.75 in near Hermon, NY
recommendations.description: Social media image of hail ranging from 2 to 2.75 inches in diameter. (BTV)
recommendations.content_angle: Actual hail report. Prioritize roof, soft metals, siding, elevations, hail size, and original-photo preservation guidance.
recommendations.posture: Preliminary SPC report. Treat as a strong signal, but label it preliminary and avoid property-specific claims.
recommendations.source: Storm Prediction Center preliminary storm reports

recommendations.headline: SPC Preliminary Tornado Report: UNK near 3 W Shavano Park, TX
recommendations.description: Video showed tornado crossing 1604. Power flashes and debris on video. Time estimated by radar. (EWX)
recommendations.content_angle: Actual tornado report. Build safety-first follow-up and post-event structural, roof, exterior, debris impact, and contents documentation guidance.
recommendations.posture: Preliminary SPC report. Treat as a strong signal, but label it preliminary and avoid property-specific claims.
recommendations.source: Storm Prediction Center preliminary storm reports

bots.next_prompt_for_main_bot: Lead with verified storm-volume signals: SPC/NWS tornado, hail, damaging wind, derecho, and tropical-wind threats. Use preliminary reports as strong but clearly labeled signals, keep active-threat posts safety-first, then move into roof/exterior/opening/contents documentation.
bots.priority_model: Hancock priority order: SPC preliminary tornado/hail/wind reports, NWS active tornado/hail/wind/derecho warnings, SPC hail/wind/tornado outlooks, hurricane/tropical wind threats, then heavy rain/flood and wildfire/smoke for contents work. Air Quality Alerts are excluded unless tied to wildfire/smoke property documentation.
sources: Storm Prediction Center preliminary storm reports

bots.next_prompt_for_main_bot: When drafting, include a short answer block, FAQ, meta title, meta description, and a Hancock CTA.

mainSpeakingBot.opening_line: I scanned the market, weather, content opportunities, and SEO/AEO signals. I have a short list ready.
mainSpeakingBot.priority: Start with Storm Watch: SPC/NWS hail, wind, tornado, derecho, or tropical-wind signals are active. Keep active-threat messaging safety-first, label preliminary reports clearly, then turn the signal into post-storm inspection documentation content.
next_steps: Start with Storm Watch: SPC/NWS hail, wind, tornado, derecho, or tropical-wind signals are active. Keep active-threat messaging safety-first, label preliminary reports clearly, then turn the signal into post-storm inspection documentation content.
Ask the user to approve the angle before drafting.
Create one draft, then repurpose it into LinkedIn copy.
Move the user toward publishing or assigning a final review task.
mainSpeakingBot.tone: Direct, helpful, task-focused, Hancock-specific. Keep the user moving without sounding pushy.
mainSpeakingBot.voice_script: I found the strongest current signal. Let’s turn one item into a useful Hancock post first, then we can repurpose it for LinkedIn.

doctrine.rule: New learning must be traceable, current, relevant, and clearly labeled by confidence.
